primary_description: Horizontal lineup of distribution-board components; A1/A2 labels are visible on a contactor-like module. Component identity and wiring implications require manual review.
review_questions: Identify each component in the lineup.
Confirm which component is the contactor and which terminals correspond to coil/control.
Decide whether this page is a component inventory or an intended wiring variant.

primary_description: Left page-2 scheme with input area, neutral bus, two breaker rows, A1/A2 contactor/control area, wall-switch graphics, and colored conductor routing.
review_questions: Confirm the role of the two breaker rows.
Confirm whether the switch graphics are part of the control circuit.
Confirm which conductors are input, neutral, control, and load-side wiring.
Ignore the crop boundary as technical evidence.

primary_description: Right page-2 scheme similar to the left variant, with added inverter phase arrows and labels for inverter input/output phase references.
review_questions: Confirm which conductor is intended as inverter input phase and which is inverter output phase.
Confirm whether red and gray arrow labels are direction markers or terminal labels.
Compare against doc_017_scheme_002 to determine what changed between variants.

primary_description: Single assembled distribution-board rework scheme with input/neutral area, upper and lower breaker rows, contactor/control module, switch graphic, and inverter phase labels.
review_questions: Confirm whether this is the clean primary topology corresponding to the page-2 variants.
Verify conductor routes before any statement about inverter input/output or reserve-group switching.
Confirm which loads or breakers are reserve-related.

scheme_title: Page 4 numbered outputs and PE/L/N legend
primary_description: Numbered-output board layout with outputs 1-6, PE bus area, and lower PE/L/N input-output legend. Exact output mapping and lower module identity require review.
review_questions: Confirm what outputs 1-6 represent.
Confirm how the PE bus, neutral bus, and numbered outputs relate.
Confirm the lower input/output device identity and terminal mapping.
Compare this variant against schemes 006 and 007.

primary_description: Numbered-output variant with outputs 1-6, visually split right-side output grouping, PE/L/N input-output legend, and an additional lower-right breaker-like module compared with page 4.
review_questions: Confirm what changed from doc_017_scheme_005.
Confirm whether outputs 2 and 3 are intentionally separated in the right-side grouping.
Confirm the purpose of the additional lower-right breaker-like module.
Verify conductor routing before promoting any output mapping.

primary_description: Another numbered-output routing variant with outputs 1-6 and PE/L/N input-output legend; visible colored conductor routing differs from pages 4 and 5.
review_questions: Confirm what routing difference this variant represents.
Compare against doc_017_scheme_005 and doc_017_scheme_006.
Verify whether the difference is a valid alternative scheme or an intermediate drawing state.
Do not promote numbered output assignments before review.
